title: Only in the Same Frame
tagline: Split a secret across two objects on opposite sides of the planet; it exists only while both sit in one camera view.
holy_shit: Either object alone is pure noise — photograph it, steal it, subpoena it, brute-force it: nothing. The message does not exist anywhere, not even encrypted-and-guessable, because half the key is simply somewhere else. Bring the two into a single camera frame and an AES-256 key reassembles in your browser and the words exist for the first time since they were split — and no server, ever, held either half or watched it happen.
powered_by: rapp-sealed/1.0 (AES-256-GCM) with the key XOR-split into two shares; /track QRs carry {ciphertext + share A} and {share B}; a /fn verify-before-exec cell reassembles the key from both shares and decrypts — entirely in the browser via native crypto.subtle. A true 2-of-2 physical secret.
agent_scenario: When a secret must have NO location until two independently-held carriers meet: XOR-split an AES-256 key into 2-of-2 shares, put {ciphertext + share A} on one carrier and {share B} on the other, and gate every read on physical co-presence — neither share alone leaks a bit, and a hash-verified /fn cell reassembles the key and decrypts entirely client-side, so no server ever holds a half or witnesses the join.
author: kody-w + Claude
